title: Security Tool Availability and Heartbeat
dataSources: Heartbeat or last-event timestamp per source type
spl:
index=_internal source=*heartbeat* OR index=main sourcetype=*
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by host, sourcetype
| eval gap=now()-last_seen
| where gap > 900
| table host, sourcetype, last_seen, gap
description: Security tools that stop reporting indicate outage or tampering. Heartbeat monitoring ensures continuous visibility and alerts on missing data.
value: Security tools that stop reporting indicate outage or tampering. Heartbeat monitoring ensures continuous visibility and alerts on missing data.
implementation: Use Splunk heartbeat or last-seen per host/sourcetype. Alert when any critical source has not reported for >15 minutes. Maintain source inventory and expected reporting interval.
visualization: Table (sources with gap), Single value (sources missing), Status grid (host × sourcetype).
knownFalsePositives: Internal CAs, dev/test, IoT, or air-gapped systems using self-signed or private PKI; govern with inventory and allowlists, not a single unscoped alert.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: All security data source TAs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Heartbeat or last-event timestamp per source type.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Use Splunk heartbeat or last-seen per host/sourcetype. Alert when any critical source has not reported for >15 minutes. Maintain source inventory and expected reporting interval.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal source=*heartbeat* OR index=main sourcetype=*
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by host, sourcetype
| eval gap=now()-last_seen
| where gap > 900
| table host, sourcetype, last_seen, gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Tool Availability and Heartbeat** — Security tools that stop reporting indicate outage or tampering. Heartbeat monitoring ensures continuous visibility and alerts on missing data.

Documented **Data sources**: Heartbeat or last-event timestamp per source type. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): All security data source TAs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal, main; **sourcetype**: *. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_internal, index=main, sourcetype=*. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap > 900` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Security Tool Availability and Heartbeat**): table host, sourcetype, last_seen, gap


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with certificate authority records, an inventory of endpoints, or `openssl x509 -text` on a sample file so expiry and subject data in Splunk match the source of truth.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sources with gap), Single value (sources missing), Status grid (host × sourcetype).
grandmaExplanation: Security tools that stop reporting indicate outage or tampering — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
